commands.review_summary:
The PR changes native hook relay CLI fallback behavior so stale or unusable direct bridge records fall through to the gateway path, and changes unavailable PreToolUse fallback to return a no-op response.

PR surface: Source -12, Tests -33. Total -45 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main fails closed for unavailable PreToolUse, while the PR diff makes that path return no-op output that Codex treats as no blocking decision.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor real behavior proof plus a maintainer/security choice on fail-open versus fail-closed semantics before repair or merge automation is appropriate.

target.title: fix(cli): make native hook relay resilient to stale bridges
target.body:
## Summary
- fall back from stale/unusable direct native hook bridge records to the gateway nativeHook.invoke path
- fail open for unavailable pre-tool relay events so tool execution is not blocked by relay outages
- keep permission_request unavailable handling fail-closed

## Verification
- pnpm exec vitest run src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts
- TMPDIR="/var/folders/fh/106677c12vb2dpynx68j804c0000gn/T/tmp.KOtcmuzV9h" pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts --testNamePattern "dead foreign|expired foreign|live unexpired foreign|stale direct bridge"
target.author: nickmartyniuk
